surface.name: Register and run an ASP.NET Core web app with Microsoft sign-in
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Register and run an ASP.NET Core web app with Microsoft sign-in. The official web-app quickstart provides a bounded route from tenant and runtime prerequisites through a completed local Microsoft sign-in flow. Prior surface before policy commit: Unresolved at Microsoft identity platform outcome, application type, and runtime selection.

alternatives_considered.name: ASP.NET Core web app sign-in quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a representative web-app path that assumes ASP.NET Core, a single-tenant app registration, and interactive user sign-in.

alternatives_considered.name: Single-page application
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer public-client path with JavaScript framework and browser redirect choices.

alternatives_considered.name: Background service, daemon, or script
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It uses application identity and client credentials rather than interactive user sign-in.

alternatives_considered.name: Protect a web API
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It defines success as accepting authorized API calls rather than signing a user in.

entry_point.developer_intent: Integrate Microsoft Entra ID and choose the identity outcome, application architecture, and runtime whose first mile should be measured.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has not supplied an application type, runtime, tenant configuration, or intended identity outcome.
The developer may not yet have a Microsoft Entra tenant or permission to register applications.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The Microsoft identity platform web-app quickstart ends when the user completes Microsoft sign-in and is redirected back to the running local app without an Entra error.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The user is redirected back to the app after sign-in without an Entra error.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The user is redirected back to the app after sign-in without an Entra error.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The user is redirected back to the app after sign-in without an Entra error.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: A Microsoft Entra tenant and an account that can access it.
Permission to register and configure an application in the tenant, or access to an existing registration supplied by an administrator.
A declared identity outcome, application type, supported account audience, and implementation language or framework.

primary_path.action: Open the Microsoft identity platform documentation.
details: The landing page separates sign-in, API protection, non-interactive API access, Microsoft Graph, external identity, and application-management work.
primary_path.input: Intent to integrate Microsoft Entra ID.
primary_path.success_signal: The available identity outcomes and application types are visible.

primary_path.action: Obtain a Microsoft Entra tenant and an account with application-registration access.
details: The representative ASP.NET Core quickstart requires a Microsoft Entra tenant and app registration.
An administrator may need to create or permit the registration.
primary_path.input: Eligible Microsoft account, organization tenant access, or administrator assistance.
primary_path.output: Accessible tenant and sufficient role permissions.
primary_path.success_signal: Microsoft Entra admin center access and the target tenant are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Tenant creation, invitation, organizational policy, or administrator approval may intervene, but the linked quickstart does not decompose those external account flows.

primary_path.action: Choose the first identity outcome to implement.
details: Documented choices include signing in users, protecting an API, calling an API without a user, and using Microsoft Graph.
External identity and enterprise application onboarding are additional Microsoft Entra surfaces.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected family matches the application goal.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The roster entry identifies only the platform, so this choice is unresolved.

primary_path.action: Choose the application type and OAuth client model.
details: Microsoft lists single-page app, web app, web API, desktop app, mobile app, and background service, daemon, or script.
The choice determines whether a user is present, which redirect or credential model applies, and how tokens are obtained.
primary_path.success_signal: The app type matches the software being integrated.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No universal app type is selected by the documentation.

primary_path.action: Sign in to a Microsoft Entra tenant and confirm that the .NET 9 SDK is installed locally.
details: The quickstart requires an Entra tenant, permission to register an application, and the .NET 9 SDK.
primary_path.input: An eligible Microsoft identity and a local development environment.
primary_path.output: Tenant access and a compatible .NET runtime.
primary_path.success_signal: The Entra admin center or Azure portal is accessible and dotnet is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A user without an Entra tenant must obtain one before continuing; tenant creation is linked but not decomposed in this quickstart.

primary_path.action: Open Microsoft Entra ID, go to App registrations, and select New registration.
details: This begins the app identity required by the local sample.
primary_path.input: Authenticated tenant access with app-registration permission.

primary_path.action: Enter a display name, select the supported account type, and add a Web redirect URI of https://localhost:5001/signin-oidc.
details: The redirect URI and account type determine which sign-ins the app accepts.
primary_path.input: The app name, intended account audience, and documented local callback URI.
primary_path.success_signal: The form contains the Web callback exactly as documented.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A different callback later produces AADSTS50011 redirect URI mismatch.

primary_path.action: Register the application and copy its Application client ID and Directory tenant ID.
details: Both identifiers are required by the generated app configuration.
primary_path.output: A registered app plus client and tenant identifiers.
primary_path.success_signal: The app Overview page shows both identifiers and they are copied.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An incorrect client ID later produces AADSTS700016.

primary_path.action: Scaffold the sample with dotnet new webapp --auth SingleOrg --name MyWebApp, then enter the MyWebApp directory.
details: The SingleOrg authentication option generates the Microsoft identity integration structure.
primary_path.output: A new ASP.NET Core web app project.
primary_path.success_signal: The MyWebApp directory and project files are created.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: SDK or template errors must be resolved before configuration.

primary_path.action: Update appsettings.json with the tenant ID, client ID, and callback path from the registration.
details: Set TenantId and ClientId to the copied values and CallbackPath to /signin-oidc.
primary_path.input: The scaffolded project and copied Entra identifiers.
primary_path.output: A project configured for the registered tenant application.
primary_path.success_signal: The Microsoft identity configuration matches the Entra registration.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An incorrect Instance or TenantId causes an invalid authority configuration; an incorrect client ID produces AADSTS700016.

primary_path.action: Run the application with dotnet run.
details: The local server hosts the configured web app.
primary_path.success_signal: The command reports that the app is listening locally.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Build, certificate, or port errors prevent the browser test.

primary_path.action: Open https://localhost:5001 and select Sign in.
details: The app redirects the browser to the Microsoft identity sign-in experience.
primary_path.input: The running local app and a tenant user account.
primary_path.output: A Microsoft sign-in prompt and redirect flow.
primary_path.success_signal: The Microsoft sign-in prompt appears, which the quick check treats as correct basic configuration.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: AADSTS50011 indicates the portal redirect URI does not exactly match the app callback.

primary_path.action: Complete Microsoft sign-in and confirm the browser returns to the local application as an authenticated user.
details: The quickstart's run-and-test section requires the sign-in flow to complete successfully.
primary_path.input: Valid credentials for an account accepted by the app registration.
primary_path.success_signal: The user is redirected back to the app after sign-in without an Entra error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Consent, tenant-policy, account-type, or configuration errors mean the first sign-in has not succeeded.

branches.condition: The goal is interactive sign-in for an ASP.NET Core web app.
branches.path: Install .NET 9, create or choose a tenant, register a web app, set the supported account audience and localhost redirect URI, copy tenant and client IDs, create the Microsoft.Identity.Web template or configure an existing app, run it, browse to localhost, select Sign in, and complete the identity prompt.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Defines success as the app displaying a working sign-in flow and returning to the local web app as an authenticated user.

branches.condition: The goal is to protect a web API.
branches.path: Register and expose the web API, define scopes or app roles, configure token validation in the selected framework, obtain a suitable access token from a client, call the endpoint, and verify allowed and rejected requests.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Moves the terminal signal from UI sign-in to an authorized API response.

branches.condition: The app is a single-page application.
branches.path: Select a JavaScript framework quickstart, register a SPA redirect URI, configure the browser application, use MSAL with an interactive flow, run the app, sign in, and inspect the returned account or API data.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses a public-client browser flow and framework-specific UI rather than a server-side web session.

branches.condition: The app is a background service, daemon, or script.
branches.path: Select a daemon runtime, register a confidential client, create and protect a credential, grant application permissions with any required administrator consent, acquire a token without a user, call the API, and verify its response.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds credential and administrator-consent gates and removes interactive sign-in.

friction_gates.description: A Microsoft Entra tenant and tenant user are required for the representative developer quickstarts.
Tenant policy and role assignments can determine whether the developer may register an application or needs administrator assistance.
The developer must choose an identity outcome before a terminal success can be defined.
Application type determines the client model, authentication flow, configuration, and verification.
A language or framework must be selected before the documentation provides atomic commands and code changes.

sources.title: Microsoft identity platform documentation
sections_used: Authentication and authorization for any app

sources.title: Quickstart: Add sign-in with Microsoft identity to an ASP.NET Core web app
Five steps to full application integration with Microsoft Entra ID

uncertainties.question: Which Microsoft Entra identity outcome should define first success?
uncertainties.impact: It changes the terminal signal from interactive sign-in to API authorization, application-only token acquisition, Graph data, or another result.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Microsoft identity platform home presents these as peer starting outcomes and the roster supplies no use case.

uncertainties.question: Which application type and runtime should be measured?
uncertainties.impact: It changes app-registration fields, OAuth client model, credentials, SDK, commands, code, and verification.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official docs explicitly require an app-type and language/framework selection.

uncertainties.question: What live screens and organizational approvals precede tenant and application access for a new developer?
uncertainties.impact: The account and permission gate cannot be decomposed page by page for every organization and tenant policy.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The representative quickstart states the tenant and registration prerequisites but does not enumerate all account-creation, invitation, policy, or administrator workflows.

excluded_after_success: No implementation-specific downstream work can be excluded until the identity outcome, application type, runtime, and terminal success are selected.
